intended_use: Imbio CAC Software is intended for use as a non-invasive post-processing software to evaluate calcified plaques in the coronary arteries, which present a risk for coronary artery disease. Imbio CAC So
intended_use_full: Imbio CAC Software is intended for use as a non-invasive post-processing software to evaluate calcified plaques in the coronary arteries, which present a risk for coronary artery disease. Imbio CAC Software uses machine learning to analyze non-contrast thoracic CT images and outputs a summary report containing Agatston score, arterial age, and calcified lesion mass and volume metrics of the calcification burden for the whole heart and individual coronary artery level. Additionally, Imbio CAC Software outputs annotated images previewing the segmentation of calcifications for informational purposes only. Imbio CAC Software is limited to the quantification of detected possible calcifications in adult patients ≥29 years of age. It does not diagnose coronary artery disease. The device output will be available to the users as part of the standard DICOM viewing workflow. The Imbio CAC Software results are not intended to be used on a stand-alone basis for clinical decision-making or otherwise preclude clinical assessment of CT images.
target_condition: Coronary artery calcification; risk assessment for coronary artery disease
ground_truth: FDA-cleared semi-automated CAC scoring software program used for manual annotation of chest CT series by experienced 3D Image Post-Processing Technologists
other_metrics: Cohen's kappa = 0.907 (95% CI 0.895, 0.920) for 5-category cardiovascular disease risk category assessment
